scripts.title: Cold Open
scripts.when: Read before anyone sits down, sets the tone
scripts.body:
U.A. High School. The most prestigious hero training institution in Japan. You are its newest students — Class 1-A, Hero Course. You are fifteen years old. Most of you have never been in a real fight. All of you have a power that makes you dangerous.

Oshiro Daikichi has read every one of your files. He is not impressed. He is waiting to be.

scripts.title: Previously on Class 1-A…
scripts.when: Read at the start of every session after the first
scripts.body:
Last time: [RECAP HERE]

You are still first-year students. You are still alive. Murano city is still standing. Whether that's to your credit or your luck — you haven't decided yet.

scripts.title: Table Rules Reminder
scripts.when: Optional — use if needed at session start
scripts.body: Quick reminders before we go: quirk use is free outside of significant physical effort unless otherwise stated. Describe what your character does, not just the mechanic. If you're not sure if something is possible, ask — I'll tell you if it needs a roll. We play to find out what happens, not to win.

scripts.title: The Entrance Exam
scripts.when: Session 1 — Oshiro addresses the class before the exam begins
scripts.body:
You are standing in a briefing room at U.A. High School. Around you are nineteen other people you do not know. At the front of the room is a man who looks like he has been awake for thirty-six hours and is angry about it.

He says: 'I am Oshiro Daikichi. I will be your homeroom teacher if you pass today's assessment. The exercise is simple: you will enter a simulated urban disaster zone. There are civilians — some real, some not. There are threats. There is no fixed objective. I want to see what you do when nobody tells you what to do.

You have ten minutes. Questions?'

He does not pause for questions. He leaves the room.

scripts.title: The Sports Festival — Opening Ceremony
scripts.when: First session of the Sports Festival arc — read as the ceremony begins
scripts.body:
The U.A. Sports Festival is broadcast live to every screen in Japan. Forty thousand people are in the stands. You are standing in the tunnel. You can hear the crowd.

The announcer's voice echoes down the concrete: 'These are the students you've been waiting for. Class 1-A and Class 1-B — the best young quirk users in the country. Today we find out who's actually good and who just tested well.'

The gates open. The light hits you. The noise is physical.

This is the part where you either go quiet or go loud. Neither is wrong.

scripts.title: The Fracture Problem
scripts.when: Opening scene — news broadcast the class sees in the common room
scripts.body:
The common room television is on. The news anchor looks uncomfortable.

'A demonstration outside the Hero Commission's Murano district office has escalated this afternoon. A group identifying themselves as Fracture — a civil-rights organisation advocating for unlicensed quirk users — has refused dispersal orders. Three support heroes have attempted to intervene. All three have been restrained without injury, according to Fracture's own broadcast.

The group's spokesperson, who goes by the name Shard, has stated: the demonstration will end when someone official agrees to talk to them.'

Oshiro walks in. He looks at the screen. He looks at you.

'Get your gear.'

scripts.title: Convergence — The Port District
scripts.when: Opening of the Convergence arc — arriving at Murano port
scripts.body:
The port district at night. Container yards, loading cranes, the smell of salt water and diesel. The tip came in two hours ago: suspected Chain operation, warehouse seven, south dock.

Oshiro dropped you at the perimeter. He said: 'Observe first. Three minutes minimum. Then you make a decision.' He didn't say what the decision was.

You are standing in the dark between two shipping containers. Something is happening in that warehouse. You can hear it — machinery, voices, something that doesn't sound like cargo.

You have been here for ninety seconds.

scripts.title: Oshiro Daikichi — Voice & Manner
scripts.when: Reference before playing Oshiro in any scene
scripts.body:
MANNER: Short sentences. No small talk. Looks at people for longer than is comfortable before responding. Never explains his reasoning unless directly asked — and sometimes not even then.

KEY LINES TO HAVE READY:
• 'Noted.' [when something impresses him — he will not say it impresses him]
• 'Why?' [when a student does something reckless]
• 'Do it again, correctly this time.'
• 'I was watching. I know what happened. What do you think happened?'
• 'Get some sleep.' [his version of 'good job']

NEVER: Raises his voice. Shows surprise. Explains U.A. policy unprompted. Uses students' first names until Tier 2.

scripts.title: Fujisaki Rei — Voice & Manner
scripts.when: Reference before playing Fujisaki in any scene
scripts.body:
MANNER: Warm but precise. Remembers everything every student has ever said. Asks follow-up questions that reveal she's been paying attention since day one. Her concern is always genuine — which makes it more unsettling when she's serious.

KEY LINES TO HAVE READY:
• 'How are you doing? Not your quirk. You.'
• 'That's interesting. Tell me more about why you made that choice.'
• 'I think you already know the answer to that.'
• 'I'll make a note of that.' [and she means it]

NEVER: Dismisses a student's feelings. Gives easy answers. Raises her voice.

scripts.title: Shard (Fracture) — Voice & Manner
scripts.body:
MANNER: Calm, articulate, believes every word she says. Does not see herself as a villain. When students challenge her, she engages the argument — she wants to be challenged, because she's thought about it more than they have.

KEY LINES TO HAVE READY:
• 'I'm not asking you to agree with me. I'm asking you to explain why I'm wrong.'
• 'A hero license says you're allowed to use your quirk. What does it say about everyone without one?'
• 'You stopped three people from getting hurt today. I stopped three hundred people from getting ignored for another year. We're not the same.'
• 'I'm not your enemy. I might be your problem.'

NEVER: Threatens. Flinches when challenged. Acts like she's losing an argument she isn't.

scripts.title: Undertow — Voice & Manner
scripts.when: Reference for the Convergence arc appearance
scripts.body:
MANNER: He sounds like someone who has already read the room, already decided how it ends, and is only still here because he finds you marginally interesting. Precise diction. No wasted words.

HE SAYS EXACTLY ONE THING TO THE CLASS:
'Good instincts. Wrong scale.'

Then he phases out.

Do not elaborate. Do not let him be drawn into conversation. He has already left by the time someone tries to respond. The line should feel like a compliment and an insult at the same time, and the players should be uncertain which one it was.

scripts.title: Level 2 — After the Entrance Exam
scripts.when: Read after the entrance exam session ends
scripts.body:
Oshiro's report is brief: 'Class 1-A has passed the entrance assessment. Placement is confirmed. Training begins Monday.'

The results are posted on the board outside homeroom. Your names are on it. You are in.

Somewhere in the building, twenty-one other students are reading the same board. Class 1-B.

You have two days before training starts. Get used to each other.

scripts.title: Level 4 — Before the Sports Festival
scripts.when: Read as the class receives their Sports Festival briefing
scripts.body:
Three months at U.A. You've run rescue drills, studied hero law, sparred against each other and against Oshiro's training drones. You've gotten better. You can feel it.

The Sports Festival notice goes up on a Tuesday. Forty schools participating. Live broadcast. Hero agencies watching.

Oshiro reads out the notice in a flat voice. When he's done, he folds the paper and looks at the room.

'You've been training for this without knowing it. Don't waste it.'

He leaves. You have two weeks.

scripts.title: Level 6 — End of Season 1
scripts.when: Read at the close of the Convergence arc — the final scene
scripts.body:
The port district is quiet now. Emergency services. Paperwork. Someone from the Hero Commission who doesn't introduce himself taking photographs of the warehouse.

Oshiro is sitting on a loading crate, reading a report someone handed him. He's been reading it for four minutes without saying anything.

He folds it. Looks up.

'Get some sleep.'

As you leave, one of you notices the heading on the report, visible for a moment before he puts it in his pocket:

UNDERTOW — CONFIRMED ACTIVE, MURANO PORT. STATUS: AT LARGE.

Oshiro is still sitting there when the last of you goes.

scripts.title: First Real Threat
scripts.when: The first time the class faces a genuinely dangerous enemy (not a drone)
scripts.body:
This is different from the training drones.

The person in front of you has a quirk and is not pulling their punches and does not care about collateral damage. Everything Oshiro has drilled into you — cover, coordination, controlling the space — matters right now.

You have about two seconds before they make the first move.

What do you do?

scripts.title: Kuma — First Appearance
scripts.when: When Kuma first transforms in front of the class
scripts.body:
You've read the file. Chain Hardskin, goes by Kuma. Quirk: progressive mass accumulation — the longer a fight runs, the larger and denser the mass becomes.

The file had a photograph. The photograph did not prepare you for this.

The ground is shaking. The containers behind him are denting from proximity alone. He hasn't moved yet.

He says: 'I'm going to make this quick.'

He is not going to make this quick.

scripts.title: Splice — First Appearance
scripts.when: When Splice is identified in a scene
scripts.body:
You almost missed her. That was the point.

She's been in this room for four minutes. She has a camera. She has already photographed everyone present. The camera is now put away and she looks like someone who is simply leaving — except she's walking toward the Chain's data terminal, not the exit.

She notices you've noticed her.

She smiles like someone who just moved a piece on a board you didn't know you were playing on.

scripts.title: Standard Session Close
scripts.body:
That's where we'll leave it.

You are [CURRENT SITUATION]. You've been awake for [X] hours. Something you didn't expect happened today.

We'll pick it up next time. Between now and then — think about what your character is worried about. Not the mission. The person.

scripts.title: Cliffhanger Close
scripts.when: End of a session that ends on a reveal or unresolved threat
scripts.body:
And that's where we stop.

[DESCRIBE THE UNRESOLVED MOMENT IN ONE SENTENCE]

You don't have an answer for this yet. That's fine. Neither do I.

See you next week.
